[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2026-03-30
[TITLE]: Three siblings who invaded neighbours' garden to hack down 33ft trees face hefty £250k 'fence war' court bill
[METADATA/INTRO]: THREE siblings could be facing a “ruinous” £250,000-plus “fence wars” court bill after hacking down their neighbours’ 33-foot trees. Robert and Amanda McCarthy had lived for years in “relative harmony” with their neighbour, Foulla Bowler, before an argument over their...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Following a trial, Judge Alan Saggerson has now ruled in favour of the couple on the location of the boundary, finding that the siblings had “trespassed” into their neighbours‘ garden to hack down the trees. 
 The trio had taken “unilateral action to re-order the disputed boundary” between the properties amid what he called “fence wars” between the neighbours. 
 The full cost of the case to the siblings will be decided later, but the decision means they are likely to be liable for the McCarthys’ lawyers’ bills, which are estimated at £130,000, on top of their own significant court costs – plus potentially as much as £115,000 in compensation.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2014-11-25
[TITLE]: Parents sue GP for millions after their son suffers devastating brain damage from meningitis - The Mirror
[METADATA/INTRO]: Parents sue GP for millions after their son suffers devastating brain damage from meningitis Ethan Wake, 9, was left with disabilities including restricted vision, problems with motor skills and communication. His parents believe this could have been avoided...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The court heard Ethan was a lively, football-mad and energy-filled boy - although with a congenital heart problem - before he was hit by the potentially fatal bug. 
 From December 2009 onwards, he had suffered with a malaise which the court heard left him increasingly listless and fever-ridden. 
 But he was able to rally on Christmas Day for his presents and be his "usual chatty self". 
 Soon afterwards, however, the symptoms returned, Mr Spencer told the court. On New Year's Eve he was suffering from constant headaches and vomiting, and intermittently screaming "like an injured animal". 
 Ethan's parents, of Ashfield Park, Whickam, Newcastle, are now levelling negligence claims against Dr Johnson, of Front Street, Blaydon. However, the GP denies all liability in the case.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: November 18th, 2010
[TITLE]: That’s me in the picture: I’m the man in the photo that captured the bailout – The Irish Times
[METADATA/INTRO]: The arrival of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) in Dublin on the evening of November 18th, 2010, confirmed the febrile rumours that the Irish Government was now so broke it would have to depend on the benevolence of strangers. The...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The shot went around the world. It was, in effect, the €67 billion photograph. 
 But who was the man who came to symbolise the bankruptcy of the Irish State? 
 I went looking for him shortly after the bailout and found his old perch occupied by a young man who claimed to be the man’s nephew. He introduced me to a man on Baggot Street who grinned to suggest he was the person in the photograph, but he had no English. 
 Three years on, and this time with a native Romanian speaker, we again found the man through his nephew. 
 Anton Rostas (42), who says he is the beggar in the famous photograph, is a father-of-five from Tileagd, a village in the northwest of Romania.
